title.en: Ontology for High-Risk Medical Devices and IVDs
title.undefined: Medical Devices Classification and Safety Ontology

description.en: This ontology models relationships between devices, manufacturers, risks, incidents and field safety corrective actions (FSCA), enabling integrated regulatory surveillance within a single semantic framework.
description.undefined:
This ontology provides a semantic representation of the medical devices domain, with a focus on device classification, safety, incidents, and corrective actions.
It is built using OWL (Web Ontology Language) to ensure logical consistency and interoperability, and it incorporates SWRL rules to support inference and reasoning.

Core Concepts

MedicalDevice:
Represents the general concept of a medical device. It is the superclass for more specific types such as ImplantableDevice or ActiveImplant.
Each device can be described through properties such as name, manufacturer, classification, and device type.

ImplantableDevice / ActiveImplant:
These classes represent specific categories of medical devices, especially those that are placed inside the human body or require an energy source to function.
They are relevant for determining risk level and regulatory classification.

RiskClass:
Represents the regulatory classification of the device, such as Class I, Class IIa, Class IIb, or Class III.
These classes are used to organize devices according to their level of risk and control requirements.

CorrectiveAction:
Represents actions taken in response to incidents, defects, or safety issues related to a medical device.
These actions help model post-market surveillance and device safety procedures.

Properties

Object Properties:
Define relationships between instances of different classes. Examples include:

hasClassification – connects a medical device to its regulatory class
hasCorrectiveAction – connects a device or incident to a corrective action
manufacturedBy – connects a medical device to its manufacturer

Data Properties:
Define literal attributes. Examples include:

hasName – assigns a name to a medical device
hasModel – represents the commercial or technical model of the device
hasSerialNumber – identifies a specific device instance
hasRiskLevel – expresses the level of risk associated with a device

Individuals
The ontology contains a set of individuals representing example devices, classifications, manufacturers, and corrective actions.
These individuals allow reasoning engines to infer new facts and validate the consistency of the ontology.

Rules and Inference
Using SWRL rules, the ontology supports rule-based reasoning to infer classifications, detect relations between device types and risk classes, and support safety analysis.
Inside Protégé, the reasoner used was Pellet.

Extensibility
The ontology is designed to be extensible and adaptable.
New classes, properties, and individuals can be incorporated as the domain evolves.

labels.en: Ontology for High-Risk Medical Devices and IVDs

comments.en: This ontology provides a formal and unified representation of the European catalogue of high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices under the MDR and IVDR regulations.
comments.undefined: This ontology is about medical devices, their classification, and the safety mechanisms associated with their use, including incidents and corrective actions.

isDefinedBy.value:
The authors of this ontology have been:

Diana Alejandrina Socola

Tomás Cuevas

abstract.value: This ontology provides a semantic model for high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices, including their manufacturers, risk classes, incidents, and field safety corrective actions. It supports formal knowledge representation, reasoning, and interoperability in regulatory surveillance scenarios under MDR and IVDR frameworks.

The Medical Devices Classification and Safety Ontology provides a framework for modeling key concepts, entities, and relationships within the domain of medical devices.
It captures knowledge about device types, regulatory classes, manufacturers, and corrective actions.
Using OWL and SWRL, the ontology supports intelligent reasoning over safety-related data and facilitates interoperability, analysis, and querying within medical device information systems.

title.value: Ontology for High-Risk Medical Devices and IVDs
Medical Devices Classification and Safety Ontology

riskDescription.value: Particulate debris from bearing surfaces causing adverse tissue reaction
Bacterial biofilm accumulation on implant surface resistant to antibiotics
Device-related infection at implantation site or systemic sepsis
Exploitable software vulnerability enabling unauthorized device access
Battery end-of-life earlier than specified, risking therapy interruption
Mechanical destruction of red blood cells by pump mechanism
IVD test fails to detect target condition in affected individual
IVD test returns positive result in absence of target condition
Thrombus formation on device surface causing embolic events
Reagent lot contamination leading to systematic diagnostic errors
Cardiac lead displacement requiring repositioning or replacement
Unintended displacement of implant from intended anatomical position
Reagent reacts with non-target analyte producing incorrect result
Unintended ionising radiation exposure exceeding therapeutic dose
Software bug causing incorrect dosing, alarming, or therapy delivery
External EMI affecting pacemaker/ICD sensing and pacing functions
Structural failure of mechanical components under physiological load
Metallic implant causing MRI image artifacts compromising diagnostics
Complete loss of electrical function leading to device shutdown
Metallic ion release from corrosion causing local or systemic toxicity

comment.en: A risk identified for a medical device or incident.
represents risk class IIb for medical devices
Represents a single-use medical device type.

intendedPurpose.value: Continuous glucose monitor for patients with diabetes
Portable patient monitor for vital signs
ICU mechanical ventilator with pressure and volume modes
Automated immunoassay analyzer for clinical laboratory
Molecular diagnostic test for SARS-CoV-2 detection
Active cochlear implant for sensorineural hearing loss
Ventricular assist device for end-stage heart failure
Large volume infusion pump with drug library
Leadless cardiac pacemaker for atrioventricular synchrony

comment.en: A medical device registered within the ontology model.
Represents a moderate severity level for a medical device risk
Represents the current status of a corrective action.
Represents an in vitro diagnostic medical device type.
Represents a safety alert corrective action type.
Represents a low probability level for a medical device risk.
Represents the type of medical device.
Represents a serious severity level for a medical device risk.
Represents a self-test medical device type.
Represents the severity level associated with a medical device risk.
Represents a serious incident associated with a medical device.

incidentDescription.value: Iluvien implant required surgical removal due to device-related endophthalmitis confirmed by vitreous culture.
ACUSON Sequoia ultrasound system froze mid-examination. Emergency shutdown required; patient examination incomplete.
Persona knee tibial insert showing accelerated polyethylene wear with metallosis confirmed at 3-year revision surgery.
5008S hemodialysis machine web interface exposed to hospital internal network. Unauthorised session accessed alarm configuration.
SYNERGY stent fracture detected on angiography 18 months post-implant causing in-stent restenosis.
Plum 360 infusion pump delivered 10x heparin dose due to software decimal point error in drug library version 4.2.1.
ACUITY X4 lead dislodgement to right ventricle requiring fluoroscopy-guided repositioning.
HeartMate 3 patient developed severe haemolysis (LDH >2000 U/L) associated with outflow graft kinking confirmed by CT.
Tivoli II cochlear implant failed to respond to initial activation programming. Sound processor replacement resolved the issue.
TRITON hip patient presented with elevated serum cobalt (18 ppb). Adverse local tissue reaction (ALTR) confirmed by MRI.
Viva CRT-D delivered inappropriate ICD therapy during MRI scan despite MRI-conditional programming. Patient experienced pain and syncope.
MAGNETOM Vida MRI caused localised skin burn (1st degree) at anterior electrode lead in patient with DBS system not declared prior to scan.
ATERA ventilator software version 2.1.3 delivered FiO2 6% lower than set value for patients in ARDS protocol. 3 patients affected.
VIDAS HIV DUO Ultra failed to detect HIV-1 group O infection in seroconversion panel. Patient missed early treatment window.
Micra AV pacemaker reached elective replacement indicator 14 months earlier than expected longevity specification.
OPTIGA CGM sensor reported glucose 3.8 mmol/L while laboratory reference showed 14.2 mmol/L. Patient delayed insulin administration.
Portico valve showed paravalvular leak grade III due to partial migration 2 weeks post-TAVI procedure.
cobas e 801 thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) assay reported false positive results (>10 mIU/L) in 12 euthyroid patients due to biotin interference.

outcome.value: Diagnostic error - false positive TSH results

incidentDescription.value: cobas SARS-CoV-2 assay failed to detect Omicron BA.2.86 subvariant in 7 samples confirmed positive by sequencing. Lot-specific issue.
Patient implanted with HeartMate 3 LVAD developed ischemic stroke 6 months post-implant. Device thrombus confirmed by echocardiography.

comment.en: A reported adverse event or malfunction associated with a medical device.
Represents an incident associated with a high-probability and serious risk.
Represents an implantable medical device classified as Class III.
Represents a user notice corrective action type.
represents the In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Device Regulation applicable in the European Union
Represents an implantable medical device type.
Represents a near miss incident associated with a medical device.

scope.value: All OPTIGA CGM sensors from manufacturing site GE-SGP-2

actionDescription.value: Enhanced calibration protocol issued. Firmware update v1.8.5 mandatory improving temperature compensation algorithm. Patients instructed to perform confirmatory finger-stick at glucose readings <4.5 or >13.9 mmol/L.

scope.value: cobas SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR reagent lot numbers CV-2024-0312 to CV-2024-0318

actionDescription.value: Immediate quarantine and replacement of affected reagent lots. Reflex testing protocol issued. All positive results from affected lots to be re-confirmed. New primer design covering BA.2.86 released.

scope.value: Micra AV lots manufactured between 2022-09 and 2023-03

actionDescription.value: Voluntary recall of affected Micra AV lots due to premature battery depletion. Replacement units provided. 97% of affected devices successfully replaced.

scope.value: All HeartMate 3 LVAD units with outflow graft lot OG-2022-*

actionDescription.value: Safety communication regarding outflow graft kinking risk. Updated surgical technique for graft routing issued. Mandatory follow-up CT at 6 months for patients with affected lot numbers.

scope.value: Plum 360 infusion pumps with drug library version 4.2.1

actionDescription.value: Urgent recall and remote software update. Drug library version 4.3.0 deployed correcting decimal point error. All units verified by biomedical engineering before return to clinical use.

scope.value: All HeartMate 3 LVAD units implanted since 2020

actionDescription.value: Safety alert issued to all implanting centres regarding anticoagulation protocol update for HeartMate 3 to reduce device thrombosis risk. INR target range revised to 2.0–3.0.

scope.value: All ACUITY X4 quadripolar LV leads

actionDescription.value: Safety communication to implanting cardiologists regarding optimal fixation technique for ACUITY X4 lead. Enhanced fluoroscopy verification protocol at implantation.

scope.value: All 5008S hemodialysis machines with network connectivity module v2.x

actionDescription.value: Security patch 3.0.1 mandatory deployment disabling unrestricted web interface access. Network segmentation guidance issued to hospital IT departments. Penetration test report provided to competent authorities.

Updated IFU regarding post-operative infection prophylaxis protocol. Extended topical antibiotic course (14 days) mandatory post-insertion. Healthcare professionals notified via direct mail and implanting centre visits.

scope.value: Portico THV sizes 23mm and 25mm from lots before 2023-11

actionDescription.value: Enhanced deployment training and updated IFU for Portico valve implantation technique. New implantation checklist mandatory for all TAVI centres.

scope.value: TRITON Total Hip ceramic-on-ceramic 28mm heads from lots TH-2020-* and TH-2021-*

actionDescription.value: Urgent safety recall due to elevated metal ion levels. Revision surgery recommended for patients with serum cobalt >7 ppb or symptomatic ALTR. Replacement implants provided.

scope.value: Persona Knee tibial inserts with lot numbers PN-2019-* to PN-2021-*

actionDescription.value: Enhanced surveillance programme: annual DEXA scan and serum particle monitoring for patients with affected tibial inserts. Patients contacted individually.
Software recall: mandatory update to version 2.2.0 resolving FiO2 calibration error. Biomedical engineers visited all affected units within 14 days. Field service documentation required.
Urgent safety notice regarding MRI scanning protocol. Updated MRI-conditional guidelines issued. Mandatory reprogramming required before any MRI scan. Programmer software update v3.2 released.
Customer field notification regarding biotin interference in TSH assay. Updated sample preparation protocol and interference screening algorithm released via software update 4.1.2.
